title: [CI-DOCS] Update license with copyright info
body:
Updates the license with copyright info.

Addresses Discussion #18646

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I've added a DO NOT MERGE label as I'd like to get someone from Legal to give this a once over.  @mheon, I think the dates are correct, please leave a comment if not.

As I commented in #18646, I think it is historically erroneous to instantiate the generic example license notice in the Apache License 2.0 appendix, but harmless and I do see a number of projects doing this nowadays. But the other aspect of the change in this pull request that I think is actually bad practice, and which I urge not be merged, is the insertion of a license notice inside the Apache License 2.0 text itself, i.e. 
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18658/files#diff-c693279643b8cd5d248172d9c22cb7cf4ed163a3c98c8a3f69c2717edd3eacb7R5-R19

@richardfontana thanks. Yeah, I'm not at all sure about the formatting or appropriate placement of the copyrights.  I've sent a mail to the Red Hat Legal team, specifically, the folks that deal with Open Source, I'll let them guide me on that.  I don't suspect this current rev will be final

>  I've sent a mail to the Red Hat Legal team, specifically, the folks that deal with Open Source, 

Haha, that would be me :)
Ooops!  Just saw the email and made the connection.  Thanks @richardfontana.
Thanks all for the discussion here, in email, and in #18646.  Given all that, I'm going to close this PR and will leave the license as it was.
